title: rubus microbelus
intro: Rubus microbelus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a type of bramble plant that belongs to the Rosaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful in treating wounds and reducing inflammation. The health benefits of Rubus microbelus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, and can be found in various forms, including fresh leaves, dried flowers, and frozen berries.
